description: River Hall Country Club is a Davis Love III Signature Design carved through the floodplain landscapes of the Caloosahatchee River corridor near Alva. The par-72 championship layout opened in 2007 and plays as part of the master-planned River Hall community. Rolling terrain, generous fairways and risk-reward par-5s define the round.

Panther Run Golf Club is the centerpiece of Ave Maria, the only university-anchored new town built in America in the last half-century. The Keith Rhebb and Riley Johns design opened in 2007 and stretches to more than 7,200 yards across wetland-framed terrain. Wide fairways and receptive greens make it genuinely accessible to public players.

Bonita Fairways is a par-61 executive course designed by Gordon Lewis that opened in 1993 within the Bonita Fairways residential community. At 3,500 yards, it rewards precision over power and suits players looking for a quick, walkable round. The course is privately owned but open to the public.

Shadow Wood Country Club's North Course is a Bob Cupp original that opened in 1999 and was subsequently renovated by Rees Jones, reopening in 2017. The layout winds through the mature canopy of the Shadow Wood community in Bonita Springs, with strategic bunkering and defined corridors that reward accuracy. It is one of three championship courses within the club.
notes: North Course original opening 1999; Rees Jones renovation completed 2017.

description: Bonita Bay Club's West campus encompasses three Arthur Hills-designed courses — Marsh (1985), Creekside (1989), and Bay Island (1994) — set within a 2,400-acre nature preserve in Bonita Springs. The Bay Island course is ranked among Florida's best private layouts. The club is strictly member-only with no reciprocal public access.
notes: West campus has three Arthur Hills courses: Marsh (1985), Creekside (1989), Bay Island (1994). yearEstablished reflects first course opening.

description: Congo River Golf is an adventure-themed miniature golf venue at 24270 S. Tamiami Trail in Bonita Springs, featuring 18-hole courses built around waterfalls, caves, and tropical rock formations. The attraction also includes live alligator feedings, gemstone mining, and an arcade. It is one of several Congo River locations across Florida.
notes: Entertainment/miniature golf venue per Rule 1. Not a regulation golf course.

description: Copperleaf Golf Club is a private, member-owned club at the Brooks community in Bonita Springs, featuring an 18-hole championship layout designed by Gordon Lewis. The course opened around 2000 and winds through preserve-edged corridors with native vegetation framing nearly every hole. Membership is bundled with residence in the Copperleaf community.

The Colony Golf & Country Club is a private club centered on an 18-hole championship course in the Pelican Landing area of Bonita Springs. Opened in 1999 and designed by Robert von Hagge, the layout features dramatic elevation changes atop an ancient shell midden ridge—rare for Southwest Florida. The club is exclusively for Colony residents and their guests.

Raptor Bay Golf Club opened in 2001 as a Raymond Floyd Signature Design adjacent to the Hyatt Regency Coconut Point in Bonita Springs. The course has since been reimagined as Saltleaf Golf Preserve, though it operated under the Raptor Bay name for over two decades as one of the region's top daily-fee tracks. The layout features wide, links-influenced fairways and substantial wetland carries.
notes: Course now operates as Saltleaf Golf Preserve. Raptor Bay name is historical/prior branding.

description: Shadow Wood Country Club's South Course is a Bob Cupp design that opened in 1998 and was renovated by Rees Jones. Like the North Course, it is reserved for Shadow Wood members and winds through the preserve-rich landscape of Bonita Springs. The layout balances challenge with playability across a par-72 routing.
notes: South Course original opening 1998; Rees Jones renovation completed alongside North Course.

description: Spring Run Golf Club is a private bundled-golf community course in Bonita Springs designed by Gordon Lewis and opened in 1998. The 18-hole layout tracks through a series of freshwater lakes and preserve areas, with water in play on the majority of holes. Membership is included with home ownership in the Spring Run community.
notes: Address sometimes shown as Estero zip but community is in Bonita Springs.

description: Highland Woods Golf & Country Club is a private bundled community club in Bonita Springs with an 18-hole championship course opened in 1995. The Gordon Lewis design is framed by the mature tree canopy of the Highland Woods residential enclave. Annual golf dues are bundled into the cost of home ownership.

Coral Oaks Golf Course is a municipally owned, Arthur Hills-designed championship course that opened in 1988 in Cape Coral. The par-72 layout stretches to 6,623 yards and is notable for its natural cypress and pine preserve corridors, unusual for a municipal track. It remains one of the most respected public-access courses in Lee County.

Esplanade Golf & Country Club of Naples is a Taylor Morrison community course designed by C.W. Golf Architecture and named one of Golf Digest's Best New Courses of 2014. The 18-hole layout in North Naples features Mediterranean-influenced surroundings and generous fairways with strategic water hazards. Membership is bundled with residence in the Esplanade community.

The Country Club of Naples is one of the oldest private clubs in Southwest Florida, established in 1966 on a Donald Ross-influenced layout at 185 Burning Tree Drive in central Naples. The member-owned club features a classic parkland design with mature trees framing tight, well-bunkered fairways. It remains a benchmark for traditional private club culture in the Naples area.

Vineyards Country Club in North Naples opened in 1988 as one of the first large-scale private golf communities in the area. The club features 36 holes of championship golf across two distinct courses, both set within a 1,400-acre master-planned community. It remains a prominent private club known for active membership and well-maintained conditions.

Cedar Hammock Golf & Country Club is a private, bundled-golf community in South Naples whose 18-hole championship course opened in 2000. The Gordon Lewis design features extensive water features and the mature hammock vegetation that gives the community its name. Membership is included with home purchase in the Cedar Hammock enclave.

Bentley Village Golf Course is a private par-3 executive course at the Vi at Bentley Village senior living community in North Naples, open only to residents and their guests. The compact layout was built in 1987 and offers a relaxed, walkable game for community residents. It is not available to the general public.
notes: Par-3 executive course within senior living community. Access restricted to residents.

description: Eagle Creek Golf & Country Club is a private, invitation-only club in South Naples whose 18-hole championship course was established in 1984. The compact, 6,000-yard layout by Ron Garl weaves through a low-density residential enclave, with mature vegetation and numerous water features in play throughout the round. Membership is selective and limited.

Naples Lakes Country Club is a private bundled-golf community whose Arnold Palmer Signature Design opened in 1999. The layout winds through the residential lakes and preserve corridors of its South Naples enclave, with Palmer's hallmark strategic bunkering throughout. Golf membership is included with every home purchase.

Naples Grande Golf Club is a semi-private Rees Jones Signature Design that opened in 2000 adjacent to the Naples Grande Beach Resort. The par-72 layout covers 6,955 yards and features the flowing, strategic style for which Jones is known, with generous landing areas and well-placed water hazards. Hotel guests and members both have access.

The Quality Inn & Suites Golf Resort hosts an 18-hole, par-72 championship course originally designed by Joe Lee and Dick Wilson, dating to 1966 at 4100 Golden Gate Pkwy in Naples. The resort-adjacent layout is open to the public and offers one of the most affordable regulation-length rounds in the Naples area. The property operates as a hotel-and-golf package destination.
notes: Hotel and golf resort. Golf course is a Dick Wilson/Joe Lee design from 1966. Yelp lists as CLOSED — verify current operating status.

description: Naples National Golf Club is a private club in South Naples whose 18-hole championship course was built in 1992 at 9325 Collier Blvd. The layout features wide, low-density corridors through preserve terrain that give it an uncrowded, natural feel. Membership is by invitation and the club has historically maintained a small, selective roster.

The Club at TwinEagles in North Naples features two championship courses: the Talon Course, a Jack Nicklaus and Jack Nicklaus II design that opened in 1999, and the Eagle Course, which opened in 2006. Both layouts are set within a 1,115-acre preserve and are among the most ecologically pristine private courses in Collier County. The club is strictly private and invitation-only.
notes: Two courses: Talon (1999, Nicklaus) and Eagle (2006). yearEstablished reflects first course opening.

description: Quail Run Golf Club is a 100% member-owned private club in central Naples that traces its origins to 1967, making it one of the older private clubs in the city. The 18-hole layout at 1 Forest Lakes Blvd is compact and tree-lined, reflecting its original mid-century design character. The club is member-owned and access is exclusively for members and guests.
notes: Some sources cite 1982 as club reorganization year; original course dates to 1967.

description: Pelican Marsh Golf Club is a private Robert von Hagge design that opened in 1994 within one of North Naples' signature gated communities. The 18-hole layout plays through wetland corridors and mature canopy with von Hagge's characteristic dramatic mounding and bunkering. Access is limited to Pelican Marsh community members.

Kensington Golf & Country Club is a private club in central Naples built in 1993 on an 18-hole championship layout at 2700 Pine Ridge Road. The course is a classic parkland-style design with mature tree corridors, well-contoured greens, and minimal flashiness. Membership is selective and the club has a reputation as one of Naples' quieter, well-run private facilities.

Bay Colony Golf Club is a private, invitation-only club within the Pelican Bay community in North Naples whose 18-hole championship course opened in 1996. The layout is set on the southern edge of the Pelican Bay preserve and is known for its tight, heavily tree-lined fairways. With only a few hundred members, it is one of the most exclusive clubs in Southwest Florida.

Hideout Golf Club is a private club in East Naples on a quiet, member-owned 18-hole course that opened in January 2000. The layout at 3025 Brantley Blvd occupies a low-density rural setting in the eastern reaches of Collier County, far from the density of coastal Naples. The club has a loyal membership that values the uncrowded, unhurried atmosphere.

Grey Oaks Country Club is one of the premier private clubs in Southwest Florida, featuring three championship courses — the Palm, Pine, and Estuary — all set within a 1,800-acre West Naples estate community established in 1993. The club has been repeatedly recognized as one of the best private clubs in the state. Access is exclusively for Grey Oaks residents who hold golf membership.

Wyndemere Country Club is a private club in central Naples established in 1981 on a 27-hole Arthur Hills design. The winding layout moves through a dense residential enclave with mature vegetation forming natural corridors throughout. The club has long been regarded as one of Naples' established, traditionally run private clubs.

Valencia Golf & Country Club is a public-access 18-hole course in North Naples that opened in 1997 at 1725 Double Eagle Trail. The layout winds through a residential community with water in play on multiple holes and a design that blends challenge with approachability for everyday golfers. It is one of few regulation public-access options in the North Naples corridor.

TPC Treviso Bay is a private TPC Network course in South Naples designed by Arthur Hills with input from Hal Sutton and opened in 2008. The par-72 layout is built to Tour standards and hosted PGA Tour qualifying events. It is the only TPC course in the Naples area.

Tiburón Golf Club at the Ritz-Carlton Naples is a 36-hole Greg Norman Signature design featuring the Gold Course (1998) and Black Course (2001), set within the Ritz-Carlton resort campus in North Naples. The layout is consistently ranked among the best resort courses in Florida and annually hosts the PNC Championship (formerly Franklin Templeton Shootout). Hotel guests and members both have access.
notes: Gold Course (1998) and Black Course (2001). Semi-private: open to Ritz-Carlton guests and members.

description: The Golf Lodge at the Quarry is the private club component of The Quarry community in North Naples, featuring an 18-hole championship course that opened in 2007 on a former mining quarry site. The layout by Hurdzan-Fry incorporates dramatic lake carries over the quarry lake, creating one of the most distinctive visual golf settings in the region. Access is limited to Quarry residents who hold golf membership.

Quail West Golf & Country Club is a prestigious private club in North Naples with two championship courses — the Preserve Course (1992) and the Championship Course — winding through a low-density estate community. The club is known for its conditioning, service standards, and the exclusivity of its limited membership. Both courses feature mature native preserve corridors.

Bonita Bay Club East is the Naples campus of the Bonita Bay Club, featuring two Tom Fazio-designed courses — the Cypress Course (1997) and the Sabal Course (1998) — set in a low-density preserve community north of Naples. Both courses carry Audubon International Signature Sanctuary certification and reflect Fazio's naturalistic design approach. Access is limited to Bonita Bay Club members.
notes: Address is 3700 Wildwood Blvd, Naples 34120. Bonita Bay Club East is the Naples campus; West campus is in Bonita Springs (34134).

description: Heritage Bay Golf & Country Club is a 27-hole bundled-golf community in North Naples developed by Lennar, with courses designed by Lewis-Azinger that opened in 2007. The layout features a series of interconnected lakes and wetland preserves and is part of a community of single-family homes and coach homes. Golf membership is included with every home purchase.

Glen Eagle Golf & Country Club is a private bundled-golf community course in central Naples that opened in 1991. The 18-hole layout winds through a residential enclave of condominiums and single-family homes with lakes and native vegetation in play throughout. Membership is bundled with all home purchases in the Glen Eagle community.

Talis Park Golf Club is a private Nicklaus Design course in North Naples that opened in 2005 and was subsequently renovated by Greg Norman Design, reopening in 2012. The redesigned layout emphasizes bold contours, strategic bunkering, and dramatic views across the 700-acre community. Membership is tied to residence in the Talis Park community.
notes: Original Nicklaus Design; renovated to Greg Norman design and reopened 2012.

description: Naples Heritage Golf & Country Club is a private bundled-golf community course designed by Gordon Lewis that opened in 1997 on a 550-acre site in South Naples. The 18-hole layout winds through a series of wetland and lake features with native vegetation preserves framing much of the routing. Membership is included with home ownership in the Naples Heritage community.

Forest Glen Golf & Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in South Naples featuring an 18-hole championship course that opened in 1999. The Gordon Lewis design moves through mature cypress and hardwood corridors with water prominently in play. Golf membership is included with every Forest Glen home purchase.

Windstar on Naples Bay is a private club in East Naples established in 1983 on an 18-hole championship course designed by Arthur Hills. The layout borders Naples Bay and Naples Bay Resort, with waterway views on several holes. The club is member-owned and access is restricted to Windstar residents and their guests.

Lakewood Country Club of Naples is a private bundled-golf community club that traces the course's origins to the early 1970s, with the club formally organized around 1978. The 18-hole layout at 4235 Lakewood Blvd is a classic low-key Florida parkland design with mature tree cover and a relaxed membership culture. It is one of the original golf communities in the Naples area.
notes: Course origins circa 1973; club established circa 1978. Sources vary.

description: Foxfire Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in central Naples featuring 27 holes of championship golf across three nine-hole combinations, with courses dating to 1985. The layout occupies a lush, tree-canopied setting with lakes framing several holes across each combination. Golf is fully bundled into the purchase of any of the 900-plus homes in the Foxfire community.

Stoneybrook Golf Club in Estero is a semi-private Jed Azinger and Gordon Lewis design that opened in 1999, set within a gated community of 1,119 homes. The par-72 layout is certified beginner-friendly by the NGCOA, with seven sets of tees offering a broad range of challenge. Daily fee play is available for non-members during off-peak seasons.

Old Corkscrew Golf Club is a Nicklaus Design public-access course that opened in February 2007 in Estero, consistently ranked among the top public courses in Southwest Florida. The 7,400-yard layout moves through wetlands and native cypress preserve with Nicklaus's characteristic strategic bunkering and green contours. It is one of the few premium public-access courses in the Lee County corridor.

Wildcat Run Golf & Country Club is a private member-owned club in Estero established in 1985 on an Arnold Palmer Signature Design. The 18-hole layout at 20301 Country Club Drive winds through the gated Wildcat Run community with Palmer's signature risk-reward architecture. The club maintains a selective membership and is not bundled.

The Club at Grandezza is a private, bundled-golf community club in Estero whose 18-hole championship course opened in 2001. The layout was designed by Gordon Lewis and features rolling terrain, lake features, and the Mediterranean-inspired architecture of the Grandezza residential community. Golf is included with all home purchases.

Stoneybrook Golf Course in Estero is a semi-private Jed Azinger and Gordon Lewis design that opened in 1999 within the Stoneybrook gated community. The par-72 layout features seven tee options and certified beginner-friendly designation from the NGCOA. Guest play is available when available via daily fee.
notes: Likely duplicate entry with Stoneybrook Golf Club-Estero (id: a7293162-7664-4ab6-a80d-eb7ece349f15). Both appear to be the same course at 21251 Stoneybrook Golf Blvd, Estero FL 33928.

description: Villages at Country Creek is a private bundled-golf community in Estero featuring a par-61 executive course designed by Gordon Lewis. The compact layout winds through the Villages at Country Creek residential community at 21180 Country Creek Drive, with lakes and tropical vegetation in play throughout. Golf is fully bundled for all community residents.
notes: Year established not confirmed in available sources.

description: Cypress Lake Country Club is one of Fort Myers' oldest private clubs, with a championship course that dates to 1959 at 6767 Winkler Road. The 18-hole layout is a classic mid-century Florida design with mature tree corridors and traditional parkland character. The club is member-owned and strictly private.

Cross Creek Country Club is a 55-plus active adult community in Fort Myers featuring an executive golf course that opened in 1985. The par-3-heavy layout at 13050 Cross Creek Blvd serves the community's resident membership and is not available to outside play. The community is designed exclusively for active adult residents.
notes: Executive/par-3 course within 55+ community.

description: Legends Golf & Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in south Fort Myers featuring an 18-hole Joe Lee design that opened in 1999 at 8600 Legends Blvd. The layout winds through a series of lakes and preserve areas with Lee's characteristic strategic bunkering. Golf membership is bundled with all home purchases in the Legends community.
notes: See also entry b6bd08b8 for 'Legends Golf & Country Club' — likely duplicate. Both are at 8600 Legends Blvd Fort Myers 33912.

description: Pelican Preserve Golf Club is a 27-hole Chip Powell design that opened in 2002 within one of Fort Myers' largest 55-plus communities. The layout features three nine-hole combinations across a naturalistic setting of wetlands and native vegetation. Semi-private access is available for non-members through Heritage Golf Group's management.

Crown Colony Golf & Country Club is a private club in south Fort Myers featuring an 18-hole championship course that opened in 2001. The layout at 8851 Crown Colony Blvd was designed with the rolling terrain and lake features typical of Southwest Florida's early 2000s private club developments. Membership is non-bundled and traditional.

San Carlos Golf Club is a private residential golf community in south Fort Myers developed in 1971 at 7420 Constitution Circle. The 18-hole layout has a traditional Florida character with mature vegetation and a low-key membership culture. It is one of the earlier planned golf communities in the Fort Myers area.

Whiskey Creek Country Club in Fort Myers is a semi-private club that traces its course origins to 1974 at 1449 Whiskey Creek Drive. The 18-hole layout has been recently reimagined and is marketed as a premier affordable golf option in the Fort Myers area. Semi-private access allows non-members to book tee times.

Estero Country Club at the Vines is a private, non-bundled club in the Vines of Estero community near Fort Myers whose course was designed by Gordon Lewis and first opened in 1984. The 18-hole layout occupies a low-density residential enclave with preserve corridors and lake features throughout. Members are free to play unlimited golf without additional per-round fees.
notes: Address is in Fort Myers zip 33967 (Estero area). Year established: course 1984; club organization 1991.

description: Myerlee Country Club is a private 55-plus community in Fort Myers whose executive course dates to 1972 at 1380 Myerlee Country Club Blvd. The par-60 layout is a walkable, low-key design serving the resident membership of the surrounding mobile home and condo community. It is not available to the general public.
notes: Executive course within 55+ community.

description: Gateway Golf & Country Club, now known as The Club at Gateway, is a private Tom Fazio Signature Design in Fort Myers that opened in 1989 at 12091 Gateway Greens Drive. Fazio's layout is consistently ranked among the top private courses in Lee County, featuring rolling terrain, bold bunkering, and mature tree corridors. Membership is traditional and non-bundled.
notes: Now officially named The Club at Gateway.

description: Fort Myers Country Club is one of the oldest golf clubs in Florida, with a Donald Ross design that opened in 1917 on the McGregor Boulevard corridor. The historic layout is operated by the City of Fort Myers and retains Ross's original strategic character, making it one of the few accessible Donald Ross courses in the state. Semi-private access makes it available for public tee times.
notes: Historic Donald Ross design (1917). Operated by City of Fort Myers.

description: Gulf Harbour Yacht & Country Club is a private waterfront club in south Fort Myers developed in the mid-1980s on a course designed by Ron Garl and Chip Powell. The 18-hole layout at 14500 Vista River Drive sits within a deep-water marina and luxury residential community. The club pairs golf with extensive yacht facilities, making it unique among Southwest Florida private clubs.
notes: Course opened mid-1980s; some sources say 1985, others 1986.

description: Shell Point Golf Club is an 18-hole championship course at the Shell Point retirement community in Fort Myers that opened in 2000. The course at 17500 On Par Blvd is available to Shell Point residents and their guests, with limited semi-private access for outside players. The layout is maintained to high standards and offers scenic views across the community's preserve and water features.
notes: Golf is primarily for Shell Point retirement community residents.

description: Legends Golf & Country Club in south Fort Myers features a Joe Lee-designed 18-hole course that opened in 1999 within a bundled-golf residential community. The layout at 8600 Legends Blvd winds through lakes and preserve areas characteristic of Southwest Florida's late-1990s golf community boom. Golf is bundled with all home purchases.
notes: Likely duplicate of entry b2c830d5 ('Legends Golf and Country Club', also Fort Myers, also 8600 Legends Blvd 33912). Both entries appear to reference the same course.

description: The Clutch is a 12-hole Beau Welling-designed short course at South Seas Resort on Captiva Island, built as part of the resort's rebuilding following Hurricane Ian and opened in late 2023. Every hole offers unobstructed water views, and the routing allows multiple lines of play on each hole. The name honors the sea turtle nesting sites found across the island.
notes: Located on Captiva Island at South Seas Resort, not in Lee County proper. Zip code 33924 (Captiva). Short course/executive classification.

description: The Club at Renaissance in Fort Myers is a private Arthur Hills Signature Design that opened in 2003 as the centerpiece of the Renaissance residential community. The par-72, 7,015-yard layout is consistently ranked among the best private courses in Lee County and is known for its conditioning and member-focused operations. Membership is non-bundled and traditional.

Burnt Store Golf & Activity Club is a semi-private 27-hole executive course in the Burnt Store Marina community of Punta Gorda, designed by Ron Garl and Mark McCumber. The course was formally organized as a club in 2008 and later transitioned to Burnt Store Marina Country Club in 2020. The three nines play to a par of 30 each, offering a quick and accessible round.
notes: Located in Charlotte County at Burnt Store Marina (Punta Gorda), not Lee County proper. ZIP 33955. Now known as Burnt Store Marina Country Club.

description: Royal Tee Golf Club in Cape Coral was a 27-hole Gordon Lewis design that closed in 2016 and was subsequently rebranded as Cape Royal Golf Club. The facility at 11460 Royal Tee Circle remains an 18-hole semi-private course open for public tee times under its current Cape Royal name. It is one of the few regulation-length public-access courses in Cape Coral.
notes: Royal Tee Golf Club closed 2016; site now operates as Cape Royal Golf Club. Verify if listing should be updated to Cape Royal.

description: The Club at Westminster is a semi-private 18-hole championship course in Lehigh Acres that opened in 1996 at 2199 Berkley Way. The layout serves both community residents and outside players seeking a regulation-length round in Lee County's inland residential corridor. It is one of the few semi-private options in the Lehigh Acres area.

Mirror Lakes Golf Club in Lehigh Acres is a public course whose origins date to 1958, making it one of the oldest golf facilities in Lee County. The layout at 670 Milwaukee Blvd SE was reorganized in 1970 and operates as a public pay-and-play facility serving the Lehigh Acres community. It remains one of the most affordable golf options in the region.

Majestic Golf Club in Lehigh Acres is a public 18-hole course dating to 1975 at 350 Homestead Road South. The flat, open layout is typical of Lehigh Acres' mid-century golf development, offering an affordable daily-fee round with straightforward design. It serves the local residential community and occasional visitors to the area.

Hibiscus Golf Club in the Lely area of Naples is a public-access 18-hole course established in 1969 at 5375 Hibiscus Drive. The course underwent an extensive renovation completed in late 2023 and reopened to the public with new tees, fairways, and greens. It is one of the few walkable, affordable daily-fee options in the South Naples corridor.

Royal Palm Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in the Lely area of Naples whose 18-hole course was built in 1976 at 405 Forest Hills Blvd. The layout is one of the earlier Lely-area courses and features a classic Florida design with mature vegetation framing tree-lined fairways. Golf is fully bundled with home ownership in the community.

Island Country Club is Marco Island's only private 18-hole golf club, with a course that dates to 1966 and was purchased by members in 1986. The layout at 500 Nassau Road is compact and tree-lined, reflecting its original mid-1960s design character. Membership is selective and the club serves as the social and sporting hub for Marco Island's permanent residents.

Hideaway Beach Club Golf Course is a private 9-hole par-3 executive course within the gated Hideaway Beach Club community on the northwest end of Marco Island, built in 1982. The compact layout at 333 Hideaway Circle North serves exclusively the club's limited membership. Access is strictly for Hideaway Beach Club members and their guests.
notes: 9-hole par-3 executive course within exclusive private gated community.

description: Club Pelican Bay is a private, member-owned club in the Pelican Bay community of North Naples founded in 1980 with three nine-hole courses totaling 27 holes. The compact nines wind through the preserve corridors of Pelican Bay at 707 Gulf Park Drive, offering a relaxed, walking-friendly alternative to the area's championship layouts. Membership is by invitation and limited.

The Glades Golf & Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in South Naples featuring 36 holes — the Pines and Palmetto courses — both B. Dudley Gray designs opening around 1975 at 174 Teryl Road. The courses are among the longest-established bundled communities in the area. Golf is fully included with home ownership.

Calusa Pines Golf Club is one of the most exclusive private clubs in Southwest Florida, with an 18-hole championship course that opened in 2001 at 2000 Calusa Pines Drive in North Naples. Membership is by invitation only and limited to a small roster. The course is a refined private layout set in native Florida upland and wetland habitat.
notes: Invitation-only private club. Very limited membership.

description: Cypress Woods Golf & Country Club is a semi-private bundled-golf community in North Naples whose 18-hole course opened in 1999 at 3525 Northbrooke Drive. The layout winds through native preserve corridors and lake features within a low-density residential enclave. Golf membership is bundled into every home purchase in the Cypress Woods community.
notes: Some sources classify as semi-private due to limited outside play; functionally bundled/private.

description: Quail Creek Village Golf Club is a private bundled-golf community course in North Naples designed by William Maddox and Gordon Lewis that opened in 1985. The 18-hole layout at 11719 Quail Village Way winds through the wooded, low-density Quail Creek Village residential enclave. Golf is bundled with home ownership and access is limited to residents and guests.

The Club at Olde Cypress is a private P.B. Dye design in North Naples that opened in 1999 at 7165 Treeline Drive. Dye's routing features strong strategic demands, with water and native vegetation framing a demanding but fair layout. Membership is traditional and tied to residence in the Olde Cypress community.

The Club at the Strand is a private 27-hole championship facility in North Naples that opened in November 1997 at 5840 Strand Blvd. The three nine-hole combinations — Sabal, Preserve, and Savannah — wind through the low-density Strand residential enclave. The club is privately owned and non-bundled, with membership by application.

Eagle Lakes Golf Club is a public 18-hole championship course in South Naples that opened in 2005 at 18100 Royal Tree Parkway. The layout is one of the area's newer public-access facilities, offering a well-maintained regulation-length round at a reasonable daily fee. It is one of few true public regulation courses in the southern Collier County corridor.

The Classics Course at Lely Resort is a private members-only club within the larger Lely Resort campus, featuring an 18-hole championship layout that opened in 1990 at 7989 Grand Lely Drive. The course was designed for the resident membership of The Classics Country Club and is separate from the public Flamingo Island and Mustang courses on the same property. Its exclusivity within a semi-public resort campus makes it a distinctive arrangement.
notes: Private within Lely Resort; Flamingo Island and Mustang are public. Classics is members-only.

description: Royal Wood Golf & Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in South Naples established in 1987 at 4300 Royal Wood Blvd. The 18-hole layout winds through a residential enclave of condominiums and single-family homes with mature vegetation and lake features. Golf is bundled with all home purchases in the Royal Wood community.

Moorings Golf & Country Club is a private, member-owned club in central Naples whose course was completed in 1963, with the club formally chartered in 1968. The par-63 layout at 2500 Crayton Road is one of the few shorter-par private courses in the area, emphasizing shot-making over raw distance. The club is exclusive and invitation-based.
notes: Course completed 1963; club formally incorporated December 1968.

description: Collier's Reserve Country Club is a private, invitation-only club in North Naples whose Arthur Hills design opened in 1993 at 11711 Collier's Reserve Drive. The 18-hole layout winds through a 224-acre natural preserve with no parallel fairways, giving each hole a secluded, natural corridor. The club is limited to 350 members and frequently cited as one of the most environmentally sensitive private courses in Florida.

Hammock Bay Golf & Country Club is a semi-private Peter Jacobsen and Jim Hardy design that opened in 2003 in southeast Naples, adjacent to the Marco Island JW Marriott. The par-72 layout was named one of Travel & Leisure Golf's top ten new private courses in the world and features live oak-lined fairways and PlatinumTE paspalum turf throughout. Resort guests and members both have access.
notes: Now owned by JW Marriott Marco Island; managed as hotel golf. Semi-private via resort.

description: The Rookery at Marco is a semi-private course affiliated with the JW Marriott Marco Island Beach Resort, featuring an 18-hole layout at 3433 Club Center Blvd in South Naples. The course, managed by Marriott Golf, is available to resort guests and members of the Hammock Bay Golf & Country Club. It is a well-maintained resort-quality track with tropical vegetation throughout.
notes: Managed by JW Marriott Marco Island. Semi-private via resort access. Year established sources conflict (1991 most cited for original course).

description: Olde Florida Golf Club is a private, invitation-only club in North Naples founded in 1992 at 9393 Vanderbilt Beach Road Extension. The 18-hole layout occupies a low-density rural setting in the eastern reaches of North Naples and is known for its natural, understated Florida character. With no residential development on the property, the course maintains an unusually tranquil atmosphere.

The Old Collier Golf Club is one of the most exclusive private clubs in the United States, with a Tom Fazio Signature Design that opened in 2001 at 790 Main House Drive in North Naples. Membership is limited to 250 and is strictly by invitation. Fazio's routing across 270 acres of wetland and upland habitat is widely regarded as his finest work in Southwest Florida.
notes: Invitation-only; membership capped at 250.

description: Riviera Golf Club is a public pay-and-play course in South Naples that opened in 1971 at 48 Marseille Drive within the Riviera Golf Estates 55-plus community. It is one of the few true daily-fee regulation courses available in the Naples city limits. The 18-hole layout offers an affordable, no-frills round in a quiet residential setting.

The Mustang Course at Lely Resort is a Lee Trevino Signature Design that opened in 1996 and is consistently ranked among the best public golf courses in Southwest Florida. The par-72, 7,217-yard layout embodies Trevino's philosophy of demanding but fair design for all skill levels. Daily fee access is available to the public year-round.

Vanderbilt Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in North Naples whose 18-hole course opened in 1999 at 8250 Danbury Blvd. The layout is set within an 800-home residential community and maintained to high private-club standards. Golf membership is bundled with all home purchases.

Golf Club of the Everglades is a private, members-only club in North Naples designed by Rees Jones on 261 acres, opened in 2000. The club was acquired by Concert Golf Partners in 2024. Jones's design features wide fairways through native Everglades-edge habitat and is regarded as one of the better Rees Jones courses in Florida.

Royal Poinciana Golf Club is a private, invitation-only club in central Naples founded in 1969 at 1600 Solana Road, featuring two 18-hole courses — the Pines and Cypress. The club is one of the older and more traditional private clubs in Naples, with a selective membership and strong emphasis on the playing experience over amenities. It remains one of the quieter, more prestigious private options in the area.

The Club at Mediterra is a private, member-owned club in North Naples established in 2000 at 15755 Corso Mediterra Circle, featuring two Tom Fazio-designed championship courses set within a 1,700-acre Mediterranean-inspired community. The North Course opened first, with the South Course following. Mediterra is consistently recognized as one of the top private club communities in Florida.

Stonebridge Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in North Naples whose 18-hole championship course opened in February 1992 at 9901 Airport Pulling Road North. The layout winds through a residential enclave of condominiums and single-family homes with lakes and native vegetation in play. Golf is fully bundled with all home purchases.

Naples Beach Hotel & Golf Club is one of the oldest golf and resort destinations in Southwest Florida, with a course dating to the 1920s and the hotel established in 1946. The Nicklaus Design renovation has kept the layout current while preserving its beachside resort heritage. The hotel and course are currently being redeveloped as Naples Beach Club, a Four Seasons Resort.
notes: Hotel closed for redevelopment as Four Seasons Naples Beach Club. Golf course status under redevelopment. Year established reflects course origins circa 1927.

description: Quail Creek Country Club is a premier private club in North Naples established in 1982 at 13300 Valewood Drive, featuring two 18-hole championship courses set within a low-density estate community. The club is one of the most prestigious non-bundled private clubs in the Naples area. Membership is by application and access is strictly limited.

Wilderness Country Club is a private, invitation-only club in central Naples built around one of Arthur Hills's earliest Florida designs, dating to 1974. Only 300 residences surround the 18-hole layout at 101 Clubhouse Drive, preserving an unusually low-density and naturalistic setting. The club is one of the more historically significant Arthur Hills courses in Southwest Florida.

Bear's Paw Country Club in central Naples was established in 1980 around one of Jack Nicklaus's early Florida designs at 2500 Golden Gate Pkwy. The 18-hole layout reflects Nicklaus's formative design period and features strategic bunkering and classic risk-reward par-5s. Membership is private and tied to the limited residential community surrounding the course.

Countryside Golf & Country Club is a private bundled-golf community in central Naples established in 1987 at 600 Countryside Drive. The 18-hole layout winds through a gated community of over 1,100 residences, with lakes and native vegetation framing most holes. Golf membership is included with all home purchases.

Hole in the Wall Golf Club is one of the most storied private clubs in Southwest Florida, with a Dick Wilson design that opened in January 1958 at 3600 Goodlette Road North in central Naples. Membership is by invitation only and the club has maintained a deliberately small, selective roster since its founding. Wilson's layout retains much of its original mid-century character.
notes: Dick Wilson design opened January 1958; year established listed as 1957 (design/founding year).

description: Lely Resort Golf & Country Club encompasses the public Flamingo Island Course, a Robert Trent Jones Sr. design that opened in 1989, and the Lee Trevino-designed Mustang Course (1996). The Flamingo Island layout at 8004 Lely Resort Blvd is the original RTJ Sr. routing on the property and remains one of the premier public-access tracks in Naples. Both courses are available for daily fee play.
notes: Lely Resort has three courses: Flamingo Island (public, RTJ Sr., 1989), Mustang (public, Trevino, 1996), Classics (private, 1990). This entry appears to reference the overall public golf operation.

description: Del Tura Golf & Country Club is a semi-private 27-hole executive facility in North Fort Myers that opened in 1982 at 18621 N. Tamiami Trail. All three nines are par-30 Ron Garl designs and are available to both community residents and outside players. The compact, walkable courses cater to the active adult community surrounding the club.
notes: 27 holes, all executive par-30 nines. Managed by Troon.

description: Six Lakes Country Club is a private 55-plus community in North Fort Myers whose course dates to 1975 at 9151 Littleton Road. The 18-hole layout serves the residential membership of the surrounding manufactured home and condo community. Access is limited to club members and their guests.
notes: 55+ community course.

description: The Palms Course is one of the public golf courses at Lely Resort Golf & Country Club in Naples, part of the Robert Trent Jones Sr.-era development of the Lely Resort campus beginning in 1989. The course is located on the Lely Resort campus at 8004 Lely Resort Blvd, Naples, FL 34113.
notes: Listed as 'Palms Course' at 'Oaks Clubhouse Drive' — most likely refers to one of the Lely Resort public courses (Flamingo Island or associated layout). Applying cityCorrection to Naples and zip 34113 per Rule 3.
